Great session last week — the 200-of-10,000 framing was exactly the kind of specificity that helps us make the case internally too.

messages.subject: Amazon Connect + Salesforce at HL — Michael Loberman suggested you
messages.body:
Charlie,

Michael Loberman suggested you're the right person — you own the Amazon Connect and Salesforce roadmap at HL.

Two things I'd like your view on:

1. Is Agentforce delivering the CC intelligence you need for agent coaching — or is it still primarily a CRM tool?

2. Under FCA Consumer Duty, how are you evidencing fair outcomes across pensions and investment conversations at scale? Manual QA on a sample doesn't evidence 100% — which is what the Duty requires.

We work with Mutual of Omaha on Amazon Connect — same stack, financial services, similar regulatory environment. 33% improvement in outcomes, QA cost halved in 6 months. I'd also mention that Declan Hourican will recognise our name from his time at TSB.

15 minutes? Happy to come to Bristol.

Marcus

talk_track.opener: Hi Charlie, Marcus Nolan from Cresta — Michael Loberman suggested you're the right person for a conversation about the Amazon Connect and Salesforce roadmap. Do you have 2 minutes?
talk_track.pitch: We sit on top of Amazon Connect and Salesforce — no replacement — and do two things: 100% call quality coverage for FCA Consumer Duty, and real-time guidance for agents during complex investment conversations. Mutual of Omaha runs exactly your stack — 33% improvement in outcomes in 6 months.
talk_track.qualifying_question: You're in the middle of the Amazon Connect transformation — is the AI coaching layer on the roadmap for this phase, or is it a separate workstream?
talk_track.ask: 20 minutes with you and whoever owns QA and compliance monitoring? I'll bring the Mutual of Omaha case study and a specific Consumer Duty framework. Happy to come to Bristol.

objections.objection: We already have Agentforce — why do we need Cresta?
objections.response: Agentforce is excellent for CRM automation — built for that. Cresta was built specifically for the contact centre: 100% call QM, real-time agent guidance during the call, conversation intelligence. Forrester named us #1. We sit alongside Agentforce — not against it.

objections.objection: We're building our own retention AI
objections.response: Streatfield's build makes sense — you want to own that data asset. The question is: does it cover 100% call QM for Consumer Duty? Most teams building retention AI leave the compliance monitoring gap open. Cresta fills that gap — additive, not competitive.

Would 20 minutes be worthwhile?

Marcus Nolan, Cresta

messages.subject: Hyperoptic CX — a question about your Trustpilot consistency
messages.body:
Emma,

I've been looking at Hyperoptic's Trustpilot reviews in detail — the headline score is strong. But the pattern in the corpus is interesting: when interactions go well, customers name a specific agent by name. When they go badly, they describe confusion, long wait times, and agents who couldn't resolve without escalation.

That gap between your best and average agents is probably costing you thousands of customers in churn you can't currently see — especially with Openreach scaling you into a million new homes this year, with a smaller team than six months ago.

Is that a fair characterisation of what you're seeing internally?

Cox Communications — same sector, similar subscriber scaling challenge — achieved a 20% revenue increase and 28.5% reduction in handle time.

Worth 30 minutes?

Marcus Nolan, Cresta

messages.subject: N Brown — JD Sports + Cox case studies, and which brand first?
messages.body:
Chris,

Apologies for the slight delay — as promised, JD Sports and Cox case studies are attached.

Based on what Simon raised in the demo, the two strongest immediate use cases for N Brown are:

1. AI Agent for WISMO and account queries — measurable containment from week one.
2. 100% auto-QM on FS arm interactions — closing the Consumer Duty gap that NICE's sampling leaves open.

I'd love a focused 45-minute 2nd session with you, Simon, and whoever owns FS compliance.

Of the three brands — which would benefit most from AI-assisted deflection first?

Marcus

talk_track.opener: Hi Chris, Marcus from Cresta — I promised you the JD Sports and Cox case studies after the demo. Have you had a chance to look at them?
talk_track.pitch: The two things that came out of the demo as strongest: AI Agent for WISMO deflection — measurable from week one; and 100% auto-QM on FS interactions — Consumer Duty gap that NICE can't close. We'd love to show both in 45 minutes.
talk_track.qualifying_question: Of JDW, Simply Be, and Jacamo — which brand has the most CC volume and the most commercial pressure to improve right now?
talk_track.ask: 45 minutes for a focused 2nd demo. Include whoever owns FS compliance and ideally Natalie Rogers.

objections.objection: We already have NICE for QM
objections.response: NICE reviews a sample post-call. Consumer Duty requires evidence on every call. Cresta + NICE are complementary — many customers run both.
That's why we start with one brand, prove ROI in the first quarter, then expand. JD Sports ran exactly this approach.
